techniques.evidence_quote: the attacker purchased the contractor's Uber corporate password on the dark web
An Uber EXT contractor had their account compromised by an attacker

The attacker then repeatedly tried to log in to the contractor's Uber account. Each time, the contractor received a two-factor login approval request... Eventually, however, the contractor accepted one
after the contractor's personal device had been infected with malware, exposing those credentials
AWS credential discovery on a shared drive
the attacker accessed several other employee accounts which ultimately gave the attacker elevated permissions to a number of tools, including G-Suite and Slack
ultimately gave the attacker elevated permissions to a number of tools
the attacker downloaded some internal Slack messages, as well as accessed or downloaded information from an internal tool our finance team uses to manage some invoices
the attacker downloaded some internal Slack messages
reconfigured Uber's OpenDNS to display a graphic image to employees on some internal sites

attack_chain.description: Attacker obtained the Uber EXT contractor's corporate password, likely purchased on the dark web after malware infected the contractor's personal device.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: It is likely that the attacker purchased the contractor's Uber corporate password on the dark web, after the contractor's personal device had been infected with malware

attack_chain.description: Attacker repeatedly attempted logins to trigger MFA push notifications (MFA fatigue) until the contractor approved one.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The attacker then repeatedly tried to log in to the contractor's Uber account. Each time, the contractor received a two-factor login approval request... Eventually, however, the contractor accepted one

attack_chain.description: Attacker successfully logged into the contractor's Uber account using valid credentials.
Attacker discovered AWS credentials stored on an internal shared drive/network location accessible to the contractor.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: AWS credential discovery on a shared drive

attack_chain.description: Attacker used discovered credentials to access multiple employee accounts and internal cloud SaaS tools (G-Suite, Slack, HackerOne).
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the attacker accessed several other employee accounts which ultimately gave the attacker elevated permissions to a number of tools, including G-Suite and Slack

evidence_chunks.quote: the attacker accessed several other employee accounts which ultimately gave the attacker elevated permissions to a number of tools, including G-Suite and Slack
the attacker accessed several other employee accounts which ultimately gave the attacker elevated permissions to a number of tools, including G-Suite and Slack

attack_chain.description: Attacker accessed internal information repositories including Slack, an internal finance invoicing tool, and the HackerOne bug bounty dashboard.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the attacker downloaded some internal Slack messages, as well as accessed or downloaded information from an internal tool our finance team uses to manage some invoices

evidence_chunks.quote: the attacker downloaded some internal Slack messages, as well as accessed or downloaded information from an internal tool our finance team uses to manage some invoices
the attacker downloaded some internal Slack messages, as well as accessed or downloaded information from an internal tool our finance team uses to manage some invoices

attack_chain.description: Attacker downloaded internal Slack messages and finance invoice tool data.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the attacker downloaded some internal Slack messages

evidence_chunks.quote: the attacker downloaded some internal Slack messages
the attacker downloaded some internal Slack messages

attack_chain.description: Attacker reconfigured Uber's OpenDNS to display a graphic image on internal sites and posted a message to a company-wide Slack channel.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: posted a message to a company-wide Slack channel... and reconfigured Uber's OpenDNS to display a graphic image to employees on some internal sites

initial_access_vector: Contractor's Uber corporate credentials purchased on the dark web (after personal device malware infection), combined with MFA fatigue/push bombing until the contractor approved a login request.
persistence_mechanism: Not explicitly documented; attacker leveraged valid accounts and discovered credentials to access multiple internal tools.
privilege_escalation: Attacker pivoted from compromised contractor account to other employee accounts, eventually using AWS/admin credentials discovered on a shared drive (PowerShell scripts) to obtain elevated permissions across internal tools including G-Suite, Slack, HackerOne.
